title: Privacy-Preserving EHR Data Transformation via Geometric Operators: A Human-AI Co-Design Technical Report
abstract: Electronic health records (EHRs) and other real-world clinical data are essential for clinical research, medical artificial intelligence, and life science, but their sharing is severely limited by privacy, governance, and interoperability constraints. These barriers create persistent data silos that hinder multi-center studies, large-scale model development, and broader biomedical discovery. Existing privacy-preserving approaches, including multi-party computation and related cryptographic techniques, provide strong protection but often introduce substantial computational overhead, reducing the efficiency of large-scale machine learning and foundation-model training. In addition, many such methods make data usable for restricted computation while leaving them effectively invisible to clinicians and researchers, limiting their value in workflows that still require direct inspection, exploratory analysis, and human interpretation. We propose a real-world-data transformation framework for privacy-preserving sharing of structured clinical records. Instead of converting data into opaque representations, our approach constructs transformed numeric views that preserve medical semantics and major statistical properties while, under a clearly specified threat model, provably breaking direct linkage between those views and protected patient-level attributes. Through collaboration between computer scientists and the AI agent \textbf{SciencePal}, acting as a constrained tool inventor under human guidance, we design three transformation operators that are non-reversible within this threat model, together with an additional mixing strategy for high-risk scenarios, supported by theoretical analysis and empirical evaluation under reconstruction, record linkage, membership inference, and attribute inference attacks.

title: Central Dogma Transformer III: Interpretable AI Across DNA, RNA, and Protein
abstract: Biological AI models increasingly predict complex cellular responses, yet their learned representations remain disconnected from the molecular processes they aim to capture. We present CDT-III, which extends mechanism-oriented AI across the full central dogma: DNA, RNA, and protein. Its two-stage Virtual Cell Embedder architecture mirrors the spatial compartmentalization of the cell: VCE-N models transcription in the nucleus and VCE-C models translation in the cytosol. On five held-out genes, CDT-III achieves per-gene RNA r=0.843 and protein r=0.969. Adding protein prediction improves RNA performance (r=0.804 to 0.843), demonstrating that downstream tasks regularize upstream representations. Protein supervision sharpens DNA-level interpretability, increasing CTCF enrichment by 30%. Applied to in silico CD52 knockdown approximating Alemtuzumab, the model predicts 29/29 protein changes correctly and rediscovers 5 of 7 known clinical side effects without clinical data. Gradient-based side effect profiling requires only unperturbed baseline data (r=0.939), enabling screening of all 2,361 genes without new experiments.

title: Boosting LLMs for Mutation Generation
abstract: LLM-based mutation testing is a promising testing technology, but existing approaches typically rely on a fixed set of mutations as few-shot examples or none at all. This can result in generic low-quality mutations, missed context-specific mutation patterns, substantial numbers of redundant and uncompilable mutants, and limited semantic similarity to real bugs. To overcome these limitations, we introduce SMART (Semantic Mutation with Adaptive Retrieval and Tuning). SMART integrates retrieval-augmented generation (RAG) on a vectorized dataset of real-world bugs, focused code chunking, and supervised fine-tuning using mutations coupled with real-world bugs. We conducted an extensive empirical study of SMART using 1,991 real-world Java bugs from the Defects4J and ConDefects datasets, comparing SMART to the state-of-the-art LLM-based approaches, LLMut and LLMorpheus. The results reveal that SMART substantially improves mutation validity, effectiveness, and efficiency (even enabling small-scale 7B-scale models to match or even surpass large models like GPT-4o). We also demonstrate that SMART significantly improves downstream software engineering applications, including test case prioritization and fault localization. More specifically, SMART improves validity (weighted average generation rate) from 42.89% to 65.6%. It raises the non-duplicate rate from 87.38% to 95.62%, and the compilable rate from 88.85% to 90.21%. In terms of effectiveness, it achieves a real bug detection rate of 92.61% (vs. 57.86% for LLMut) and improves the average Ochiai coefficient from 25.61% to 38.44%. For fault localization, SMART ranks 64 more bugs as Top-1 under MUSE and 57 more under Metallaxis.

title: Evaluating a Multi-Agent Voice-Enabled Smart Speaker for Care Homes: A Safety-Focused Framework
abstract: Artificial intelligence (AI) is increasingly being explored in health and social care to reduce administrative workload and allow staff to spend more time on patient care. This paper evaluates a voice-enabled Care Home Smart Speaker designed to support everyday activities in residential care homes, including spoken access to resident records, reminders, and scheduling tasks. A safety-focused evaluation framework is presented that examines the system end-to-end, combining Whisper-based speech recognition with retrieval-augmented generation (RAG) approaches (hybrid, sparse, and dense). Using supervised care-home trials and controlled testing, we evaluated 330 spoken transcripts across 11 care categories, including 184 reminder-containing interactions. These evaluations focus on (i) correct identification of residents and care categories, (ii) reminder recognition and extraction, and (iii) end-to-end scheduling correctness under uncertainty (including safe deferral/clarification). Given the safety-critical nature of care homes, particular attention is also paid to reliability in noisy environments and across diverse accents, supported by confidence scoring, clarification prompts, and human-in-the-loop oversight. In the best-performing configuration (GPT-5.2), resident ID and care category matching reached 100% (95% CI: 98.86-100), while reminder recognition reached 89.09\% (95% CI: 83.81-92.80) with zero missed reminders (100% recall) but some false positives. End-to-end scheduling via calendar integration achieved 84.65% exact reminder-count agreement (95% CI: 78.00-89.56), indicating remaining edge cases in converting informal spoken instructions into actionable events. The findings suggest that voice-enabled systems, when carefully evaluated and appropriately safeguarded, can support accurate documentation, effective task management, and trustworthy use of AI in care home settings.

title: Spatial multi-omics of multiple myeloma uncovers niche-dependent pro-myeloma and immunosuppressive signaling in the bone marrow and extramedullary lesions
abstract: Multiple myeloma (MM) is a plasma cell malignancy shaped by dynamic interactions between MM cells and non-malignant cells in the immune microenvironment. To spatially profile the influence of cellular context on MM and immune cell expression, we developed a multimodal framework integrating 10x Genomics Visium HD, 10x Genomics Xenium, and clinically annotated single-cell RNA (scRNA-seq) sequencing datasets. Visium HD enabled unbiased, whole transcriptome, spatial discovery at 16 m resolution, Xenium provided orthogonal validation at single-cell resolution, and scRNA-seq extended findings by mapping spatial labels and leveraging the greater sequencing depth. We developed a custom framework for cell type annotation within Visium HD spatial bins. Our approach enabled identification of plasma cell-dense niches enriched for non-canonical Wnt signaling, associated with gene expression supporting cell adhesion mediated drug resistance, inferior progression-free survival, and extramedullary lesions. Immune cells within these neighborhoods exhibited suppressed transcriptional states, including increased inhibitory receptor expression such as LAG3. Utilizing the niche-driven transcriptional states in MM and immune cells, we were able to develop a 15-gene signature independently predictive of progression free survival (HR = 2.00, p < 0.0001). Collectively, this study demonstrates the potential of integrated spatial and single-cell transcriptomics to define niche-specific programs supporting MM progression.

title: Cross-Slice Knowledge Transfer via Masked Multi-Modal Heterogeneous Graph Contrastive Learning for Spatial Gene Expression Inference
abstract: While spatial transcriptomics (ST) has advanced our understanding of gene expression in tissue context, its high experimental cost limits its large-scale application. Predicting ST from pathology images is a promising, cost-effective alternative, but existing methods struggle to capture complex cross-slide spatial relationships. To address the challenge, we propose SpaHGC, a multi-modal heterogeneous graph-based model that captures both intra-slice and inter-slice spot-spot relationships from histology images. It integrates local spatial context within the target slide and cross-slide similarities computed from image embeddings extracted by a pathology foundation model. These embeddings enable inter-slice knowledge transfer, and SpaHGC further incorporates Masked Graph Contrastive Learning to enhance feature representation and transfer spatial gene expression knowledge from reference to target slides, enabling it to model complex spatial dependencies and significantly improve prediction accuracy. We conducted comprehensive benchmarking on seven matched histology-ST datasets from different platforms, tissues, and cancer subtypes. The results demonstrate that SpaHGC significantly outperforms the existing nine state-of-the-art methods across all evaluation metrics. Additionally, the predictions are significantly enriched in multiple cancer-related pathways, thereby highlighting its strong biological relevance and application potential.

title: AI-readiness for Biomedical Data
abstract: Biomedical research is rapidly adopting artificial intelligence (AI). Yet the inherent complexity of biomedical data preparation requires implementing actionable, robust criteria for ethical and explainable AI (XAI) at the "pre-model" stage, encompassing data acquisition, detailed transformations, and ethical governance. Simple conformance to FAIR (Findable, Accessible, Interoperable, Reusable) Principles is insufficient. Here, we define criteria and practices for reliable AI-readiness of biomedical data, developed by the NIH Bridge to Artificial Intelligence (Bridge2AI) Standards Working Group across seven core dimensions of dataset AI-readiness: FAIRness, Provenance, Characterization, Ethics, Pre-model Explainability, Sustainability, and Computability. Conformance to these criteria provides a basis for pre-model scientific rigor and ethical integrity, mitigating downstream risks of bias and error before AI modeling. We apply and evaluate these standards across all four Bridge2AI flagship datasets, spanning functional genomics to clinical medicine, and encode them in machine-actionable metadata bound to the datasets. This framework sets a benchmark for preparing ethical, reusable datasets in biomedical AI and provides standardized methods for reliable pre-model data evaluation.
abstract_zh: 生物医学研究正迅速转向人工智能（AI）驱动模式，但生物医学数据准备的复杂性要求在“模型前”阶段（涵盖数据获取、转换与伦理治理）实施稳健且可操作的标准。本研究指出，单纯遵循 FAIR 原则已不足以满足伦理和可解释 AI（XAI）的需求。为此，NIH Bridge2AI 标准工作组定义了生物医学数据“AI 就绪度”（AI-readiness）的准则与实践，涵盖七个核心维度：FAIR性、溯源性、特征化、伦理、模型前可解释性、可持续性和计算性。遵循这些准则可确保模型前的科学严谨性与伦理完整性，在建模前降低偏见和错误风险。研究人员在涵盖功能基因组学到临床医学的四个 Bridge2AI 旗舰数据集上应用并评估了这些标准，并将其编码为机器可操作的元数据。该框架为构建伦理、可重用的生物医学 AI 数据集设定了基准，并提供了标准化的数据评估方法。
why_it_matters: 这篇工作偏数据集或基准构建，适合判断是否能作为病原组学训练或评测资源。
analysis_zh:
## 核心内容
该研究由 NIH Bridge2AI 标准工作组主导，旨在解决生物医学数据在进入 AI 建模阶段前的标准化问题。核心定义了“AI 就绪度”（AI-readiness）的七大维度：FAIR性（可发现、可访问、可互操作、可重用）、溯源性（Provenance）、特征化（Characterization）、伦理（Ethics）、模型前可解释性（Pre-model Explainability）、可持续性（Sustainability）和计算性（Computability）。

## 为什么相关
在病原组学、蛋白质组学和临床微生物学领域，高质量的标注数据是训练精准模型的瓶颈。该工作提出的“模型前”评估框架，直接关联到如何筛选和处理高质量的基因组或临床微生物数据集，以确保其能被 AI 模型有效利用，而非仅仅是存储在数据库中。对于构建病原体致病性预测或耐药性分析模型的研究者，该框架提供了评估数据质量的底层逻辑。

## 新颖度判断
新颖度较高（6.25）。其创新点在于明确提出“单纯遵循 FAIR 原则不足以支撑 AI 需求”，并首次系统性地引入了“模型前可解释性”和“计算性”作为数据准备的硬性指标。这标志着生物医学数据管理从“以存储为中心”向“以 AI 训练为中心”的范式转变。

## 方法与证据
研究方法包括制定七个维度的评价准则，并将其应用于四个 Bridge2AI 旗舰数据集进行实证评估。这些数据集跨度很大，从功能基因组学（Functional Genomics）到临床医学。证据体现为将这些标准成功编码为“机器可操作的元数据”（machine-actionable metadata），并与数据集绑定，证明了标准的可实施性。

## 应用价值
该框架为生物医学 AI 领域提供了数据准备的“说明书”。对于病原组学研究者，可以利用该标准评估公共数据库（如 NCBI SRA 或 PDB）中的数据是否具备足够的 AI 就绪度。此外，它有助于在数据产生阶段就规避潜在的伦理偏见和计算兼容性问题，提高下游 AI 模型的鲁棒性。

## 局限与风险
摘要未说明具体的量化评分机制，即如何定义某个数据集在特定维度（如“计算性”）上是否达标。此外，虽然在四个旗舰数据集上进行了验证，但对于数据异构性极强的临床微生物学实时监测数据，该标准的适配成本和复杂性尚未在摘要中体现。目前尚不清楚遵循这些标准是否能直接提升下游 AI 模型的预测性能指标。

title: Fingerprinting Fluorescent In Situ Hybridization Enables Multiplexed Identification of Pathogenic Bacteria.
abstract: Fluorescence in situ hybridization (FISH) is a highly specific technique for pathogenic bacteria detection that requires no culturing and provides simultaneous information on pathogenic bacteria abundance, morphology, and spatial localization. However, the limited sensitivity and poor multiplexing capacity of conventional FISH have hindered its broader application. Herein, we proposed a fingerprinting FISH (FinFISH) strategy driven by DNA self-assembly for multiplexed pathogenic bacteria detection. Using respiratory pathogens as representative models, FinFISH employs three distinct fluorophores in combinatorial labeling to generate identifiable fluorescent fingerprints for each species. In this work, FAM, Cy3, and Cy5 were selected as the fluorescent reporters because they represent well-established fluorophore combinations for multicolor imaging and combinatorial encoding, with minimal spectral overlap under standard fluorescence microscopy conditions. This strategy enables pathogen detection far beyond the limitations imposed by fluorescence channel numbers, effectively overcoming the throughput bottleneck of conventional imaging systems while offering high scalability for further expansion. Additionally, a custom-designed enclosed chip featuring multichannel reaction chambers improves parallel sample processing and simplifies experimental operation. Experimental results demonstrated that FinFISH not only performed well in identifying pathogenic bacteria within simulated sputum and urine samples but also proved applicable to clinical samples. Moreover, FinFISH provides additional semiquantitative insights into mixed infections. With future integration of expanded probe design and artificial intelligence (AI)-assisted analysis, FinFISH has the potential to advance clinical pathogenic bacteria diagnostics, microbial colocalization studies, and spatial analysis of intratumoral bacteria.
abstract_zh: 荧光原位杂交（FISH）是一种无需培养即可检测病原菌的高特异性技术，能同时提供病原菌丰度、形态和空间定位信息。然而，传统 FISH 灵敏度低且多重检测能力有限，限制了其广泛应用。本研究提出了一种由 DNA 自组装驱动的指纹荧光原位杂交（FinFISH）策略，用于多重病原菌检测。以呼吸道病原体为模型，FinFISH 利用 FAM、Cy3 和 Cy5 三种荧光团进行组合标记，为每种病原体生成独特的荧光指纹。该策略突破了荧光通道数量对检测通量的限制，克服了传统成像系统的瓶颈，具有良好的扩展性。此外，研究设计了具有多通道反应室的封闭式芯片，简化了操作并提高了并行处理能力。实验结果显示，FinFISH 在模拟痰液、尿液及临床样本中均表现出良好的识别性能，并能对混合感染进行半定量分析。未来结合 AI 辅助分析，该技术有望推动临床病原菌诊断和空间微生物组学研究。
why_it_matters: 这篇工作更接近临床/监测落地，适合评估其对快速识别、预警或治疗辅助的实际价值。
analysis_zh:
## 核心内容
该研究开发了一种名为 FinFISH（指纹荧光原位杂交）的新型检测策略。其核心是通过 DNA 自组装技术，利用 FAM、Cy3 和 Cy5 三种标准荧光团的组合标记（Combinatorial Labeling），为不同的病原菌生成特异性的“荧光指纹”。这种方法旨在解决传统 FISH 技术在多重检测（Multiplexing）时受限于显微镜物理通道数量的问题，并配套开发了多通道封闭式芯片以优化实验流程。

## 为什么相关
在临床微生物学领域，快速且多重的病原体识别是诊断混合感染的关键。FinFISH 能够直接在痰液和尿液等临床样本中工作，且无需培养，这符合临床对快速诊断的需求。对于病原生物信息学领域，该技术提供的空间定位和半定量数据为研究微生物群落结构和种间相互作用提供了高质量的底层数据。

## 新颖度判断
新颖度中等偏上。其创新点不在于发现新的生物标志物，而在于编码策略的改进：通过 DNA 自组装驱动的组合编码，使有限的荧光通道能够识别超出通道数量的病原体种类。相比于传统的 1:1 探针标记，这种“指纹”概念显著提升了成像检测的通量上限。

## 方法与证据
研究采用呼吸道病原体作为模型生物。实验证据包括：1. 使用 FAM、Cy3、Cy5 三种荧光团在标准荧光显微镜下实现了多重编码；2. 在模拟样本（痰液、尿液）中验证了识别准确性；3. 进行了临床样本实测；4. 证明了该系统对混合感染具有半定量分析能力。此外，研究还展示了定制化封闭芯片在简化操作和并行处理方面的有效性。

## 应用价值
该技术在临床病原菌快速诊断、微生物空间共定位研究以及肿瘤内细菌的空间分析（Spatial Analysis）中具有直接应用价值。其高扩展性意味着通过增加探针组合，可以进一步扩大检测谱，适用于复杂感染环境的现场筛查。

## 局限与风险
1. **AI 交叉程度弱**：摘要中明确指出 AI 辅助分析属于“未来集成（Future integration）”方向，目前阶段尚未展示具体的 AI 算法模型或自动化识别逻辑。2. **灵敏度数据模糊**：虽然提到传统 FISH 灵敏度低，但摘要未给出 FinFISH 具体的检测限（LOD）指标。3. **探针设计复杂性**：随着检测目标增加，DNA 自组装探针的组合设计与杂交动力学控制可能变得复杂，摘要未说明在大规模多重检测下的串扰处理细节。

title: BeliefShift: Benchmarking Temporal Belief Consistency and Opinion Drift in LLM Agents
abstract: LLMs are increasingly used as long-running conversational agents, yet every major benchmark evaluating their memory treats user information as static facts to be stored and retrieved. That's the wrong model. People change their minds, and over extended interactions, phenomena like opinion drift, over-alignment, and confirmation bias start to matter a lot. BeliefShift introduces a longitudinal benchmark designed specifically to evaluate belief dynamics in multi-session LLM interactions. It covers three tracks: Temporal Belief Consistency, Contradiction Detection, and Evidence-Driven Revision. The dataset includes 2,400 human-annotated multi-session interaction trajectories spanning health, politics, personal values, and product preferences. We evaluate seven models including GPT-4o, Claude 3.5 Sonnet, Gemini 1.5 Pro, LLaMA-3, and Mistral-Large under zero-shot and retrieval-augmented generation (RAG) settings. Results reveal a clear trade-off: models that personalize aggressively resist drift poorly, while factually grounded models miss legitimate belief updates. We further introduce four novel evaluation metrics: Belief Revision Accuracy (BRA), Drift Coherence Score (DCS), Contradiction Resolution Rate (CRR), and Evidence Sensitivity Index (ESI).
why_it_matters: 这篇工作偏数据集或基准构建，适合判断是否能作为病原组学训练或评测资源。

title: CVPD at QIAS 2026: RAG-Guided LLM Reasoning for Al-Mawarith Share Computation and Heir Allocation
abstract: Islamic inheritance (Ilm al-Mawarith) is a multi-stage legal reasoning task requiring the identification of eligible heirs, resolution of blocking rules (hajb), assignment of fixed and residual shares, handling of adjustments such as awl and radd, and generation of a consistent final distribution. The task is further complicated by variations across legal schools and civil-law codifications, requiring models to operate under explicit legal configurations. We present a retrieval-augmented generation (RAG) pipeline for this setting, combining rule-grounded synthetic data generation, hybrid retrieval (dense and BM25) with cross-encoder reranking, and schema-constrained output validation. A symbolic inheritance calculator is used to generate a large high-quality synthetic corpus with full intermediate reasoning traces, ensuring legal and numerical consistency. The proposed system achieves a MIR-E score of 0.935 and ranks first on the official QIAS 2026 blind-test leaderboard. Results demonstrate that retrieval-grounded, schema-aware generation significantly improves reliability in high-precision Arabic legal reasoning tasks.
why_it_matters: 这篇工作偏基础模型/Agent方向，可能影响病原检测任务的建模上限，值得关注其任务定义与评测设计。

title: CanViT: Toward Active-Vision Foundation Models
abstract: Active computer vision promises efficient, biologically plausible perception through sequential, localized glimpses, but lacks scalable general-purpose architectures and pretraining pipelines. As a result, Active-Vision Foundation Models (AVFMs) have remained unexplored. We introduce CanViT, the first task- and policy-agnostic AVFM. CanViT uses scene-relative RoPE to bind a retinotopic Vision Transformer backbone and a spatiotopic scene-wide latent workspace, the canvas. Efficient interaction with this high-capacity working memory is supported by Canvas Attention, a novel asymmetric cross-attention mechanism. We decouple thinking (backbone-level) and memory (canvas-level), eliminating canvas-side self-attention and fully-connected layers to achieve low-latency sequential inference and scalability to large scenes. We propose a label-free active vision pretraining scheme, policy-agnostic passive-to-active dense latent distillation: reconstructing scene-wide DINOv3 embeddings from sequences of low-resolution glimpses with randomized locations, zoom levels, and lengths. We pretrain CanViT-B from a random initialization on 13.2 million ImageNet-21k scenes -- an order of magnitude more than previous active models -- and 1 billion random glimpses, in 166 hours on a single H100. On ADE20K segmentation, a frozen CanViT-B achieves 38.5% mIoU in a single low-resolution glimpse, outperforming the best active model's 27.6% with 19.5x fewer inference FLOPs and no fine-tuning, as well as its FLOP- or input-matched DINOv3 teacher. Given additional glimpses, CanViT-B reaches 45.9% ADE20K mIoU. On ImageNet-1k classification, CanViT-B reaches 81.2% top-1 accuracy with frozen teacher probes. CanViT generalizes to longer rollouts, larger scenes, and new policies. Our work closes the wide gap between passive and active vision on semantic segmentation and demonstrates the potential of AVFMs as a new research axis.
why_it_matters: 这篇工作偏基础模型/Agent方向，可能影响病原检测任务的建模上限，值得关注其任务定义与评测设计。

title: Parametric Knowledge and Retrieval Behavior in RAG Fine-Tuning for Electronic Design Automation
abstract: Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) fine-tuning has shown substantial improvements over vanilla RAG, yet most studies target document question answering and often rely on standard NLP metrics that can obscure factual differences. We evaluate RAG fine-tuning for long-form text generation in electronic design automation, adapting a 7B model under five context augmentation strategies with varying retrieval conditions. We introduce TriFEX, a human-validated, triple-based evaluation pipeline that attributes generated claims to their origin-user query, context and reference-and propose Parametric Knowledge Precision (PKP), which isolates internalized knowledge by filtering out claims leaked in the prompt. We show that ROUGE and BERTScore fail to detect factual differences that our triple-based evaluation reveals. Additionally, we demonstrate that an existing metric for knowledge internalization is retrieva-sensitive, with about 75% of its cross-condition variance driven by changes in the rate at which internal knowledge is expressed (PR), rather than by changes in its actual correctness (PKP). The fine-tuned 7B variants outperform a 72B baseline on most metrics, further showing generalization across conditions and on a related benchmark. These results underscore the limitations of available metrics in RAG evaluation and show that smaller models could be reasonably well adapted to specialized tasks for cost-efficient, on-premises deployment.
why_it_matters: 这篇工作偏数据集或基准构建，适合判断是否能作为病原组学训练或评测资源。

title: An Industrial-Scale Retrieval-Augmented Generation Framework for Requirements Engineering: Empirical Evaluation with Automotive Manufacturing Data
abstract: Requirements engineering in Industry 4.0 faces critical challenges with heterogeneous, unstructured documentation spanning technical specifications, supplier lists, and compliance standards. While retrieval-augmented generation (RAG) shows promise for knowledge-intensive tasks, no prior work has evaluated RAG on authentic industrial RE workflows using comprehensive production-grade performance metrics. This paper presents a comprehensive empirical evaluation of RAG for industrial requirements engineering automation using authentic automotive manufacturing documentation comprising 669 requirements across four specification standards (MBN 9666-1, MBN 9666-2, BQF 9666-5, MBN 9666-9) spanning 2015-2023, plus 49 supplier qualifications with extensive supporting documentation. Through controlled comparisons with BERT-based and ungrounded LLM approaches, the framework achieves 98.2% extraction accuracy with complete traceability, outperforming baselines by 24.4% and 19.6%, respectively. Hybrid semantic-lexical retrieval achieves MRR of 0.847. Expert quality assessment averaged 4.32/5.0 across five dimensions. The evaluation demonstrates 83% reduction in manual analysis time and 47% cost savings through multi-provider LLM orchestration. Ablation studies quantify individual component contributions. Longitudinal analysis reveals a 55% reduction in requirement volume coupled with 1,800% increase in IT security focus, identifying 10 legacy suppliers (20.4%) requiring requalification, representing potential $2.3M in avoided contract penalties.
why_it_matters: 这篇工作偏基础模型/Agent方向，可能影响病原检测任务的建模上限，值得关注其任务定义与评测设计。

title: AI Co-Scientist for Ranking: Discovering Novel Search Ranking Models alongside LLM-based AI Agents with Cloud Computing Access
abstract: Recent advances in AI agents for software engineering and scientific discovery have demonstrated remarkable capabilities, yet their application to developing novel ranking models in commercial search engines remains unexplored. In this paper, we present an AI Co-Scientist framework that automates the full search ranking research pipeline: from idea generation to code implementation and GPU training job scheduling with expert in the loop. Our approach strategically employs single-LLM agents for routine tasks while leveraging multi-LLM consensus agents (GPT 5.2, Gemini Pro 3, and Claude Opus 4.5) for challenging phases such as results analysis and idea generation. To our knowledge, this is the first study in the ranking community to utilize an AI Co-Scientist framework for algorithmic research. We demonstrate that this framework discovered a novel technique for handling sequence features, with all model enhancements produced automatically, yielding substantial offline performance improvements. Our findings suggest that AI systems can discover ranking architectures comparable to those developed by human experts while significantly reducing routine research workloads.
abstract_zh: 本文介绍了一种 AI 协同科学家（AI Co-Scientist）框架，旨在自动化商业搜索引擎中新型排序模型的研发全流程，涵盖从创意生成、代码实现到 GPU 训练任务调度的环节。该框架在常规任务中采用单 LLM 智能体，而在结果分析和创意生成等挑战性阶段，则利用多 LLM 共识智能体（集成 GPT 5.2、Gemini Pro 3 和 Claude Opus 4.5）。据知，这是排序领域首个利用 AI 协同科学家框架进行算法研究的工作。实验证明，该框架自动发现了一种处理序列特征的新技术，所有模型增强均由系统自动产出，并显著提升了离线性能指标。研究结果表明，AI 系统能够发现与人类专家水平相当的排序架构，同时大幅减轻了常规研究的工作负担。
why_it_matters: 这篇工作更像方法创新，可能直接关联 metagenomics、long-read 或 pathogen identification 流程优化。
analysis_zh:
## 核心内容
该研究开发了一个名为“AI Co-Scientist”的自动化框架，专门用于搜索引擎排序算法的研发。该框架通过集成多种前沿大语言模型（GPT 5.2、Gemini Pro 3、Claude Opus 4.5），实现了从理论构思到代码编写、再到云端 GPU 算力调度的全流程自动化。其核心在于利用多模型共识机制来处理复杂的科研决策任务。

## 为什么相关
**弱 AI 交叉相关。** 虽然该论文的直接应用场景是“商业搜索引擎排序”，但其核心方法论——即利用 AI Agent 自动化算法迭代和特征工程，与病原微生物学中的宏基因组数据分析、长读段测序（Long-read）序列比对优化以及病原体鉴定流程的参数调优具有高度的方法论相似性。这种自动化科研范式可被迁移至生物信息学算法的开发中。

## 新颖度判断
**中等偏高。** 该研究的新颖性在于其是排序算法领域首个完整的“AI 科学家”闭环系统。特别是在技术栈上，它率先采用了尚未广泛普及的下一代模型（如摘要提到的 GPT 5.2 等）进行多模型共识决策，而非单一模型生成。

## 方法与证据
- **方法：** 采用单 LLM 处理常规任务，多 LLM（GPT 5.2, Gemini Pro 3, Claude Opus 4.5）共识机制处理核心科研环节（分析与构思）。
- **证据：** 框架自动发现了一种全新的“序列特征处理技术”。
- **指标：** 摘要提到该技术在离线测试中取得了“显著的性能提升”（substantial offline performance improvements），且生成的架构可与人类专家设计的模型相媲美。

## 应用价值
对于生物信息学领域，该框架的逻辑可用于自动探索更优的基因组组装算法或蛋白质结构预测模型。它能显著降低科研人员在编写底层代码和手动调参上的时间投入，将精力集中于高层级的生物学假设验证。

## 局限与风险
- **领域局限：** 摘要明确指出该研究目前仅限于“商业搜索引擎”和“排序模型”，并未在生物信息学或临床微生物学数据上进行验证。
- **成本风险：** 调用 GPT 5.2 等多模型共识机制可能产生高昂的 API 成本。
- **透明度：** 摘要未说明自动发现的“序列特征技术”的具体数学原理，存在黑盒发现的风险。

title: Design Space and Implementation of RAG-Based Avatars for Virtual Archaeology
abstract: Immersive technologies, such as virtual and augmented reality, are transforming digital heritage by enabling users to explore and interact with culturally significant sites. It is now possible to view and augment digital twins, or digitally reconstructed versions of them, and to enable access to previously unreachable locations for a broader audience. Here, we investigate retrieval-augmented generation (RAG)-based avatars as an interface for accessing further information about digital cultural heritage objects while immersed in dedicated virtual environments. We present a requirement design space that spans the application realm, avatar personality, and I/O modalities. We instantiate it with a RAG system coupled to a conversational avatar in a virtual reality (VR) environment, using the Maxentius mausoleum from the 4th century AD as a case study, through which users gain access to curated on-demand information of the digitised heritage object. Our workflow utilises scholarly texts and enriches them with metadata. We evaluate various RAG configurations in terms of answer quality on a small expert-crafted question-answer set, as well as the perceived workload of users of a VR setup using such a RAG avatar. We demonstrate evidence that users perceive the overall workload for interacting with such an avatar as below average and that such avatars help to gain topical engagement. Overall, our work demonstrates how to utilise RAG-driven VR avatars for archaeological purposes and provides evidence that they can offer a pathway for immersive, AI-enhanced digital heritage applications.
abstract_zh: 沉浸式技术（如虚拟现实和增强现实）正在改变数字遗产领域，使用户能够探索具有文化意义的遗址。本研究探讨了基于检索增强生成（RAG）的虚拟化身，将其作为在虚拟环境中获取数字文化遗产对象信息的交互界面。我们提出了一个涵盖应用领域、化身个性和输入/输出模式的需求设计空间。研究以公元4世纪的马森提乌斯陵墓（Maxentius mausoleum）为案例，在虚拟现实（VR）环境中实现了一个耦合对话化身的RAG系统，使用户能够按需获取经过策划的数字化遗产信息。该工作流利用学术文本并辅以元数据。我们通过专家设计的问答集评估了不同RAG配置的回答质量，并测量了用户在VR环境中使用该化身时的感知工作量。结果表明，用户与此类化身交互的整体工作量低于平均水平，且有助于提高对主题的参与度。本研究展示了如何将RAG驱动的VR化身应用于考古学，为沉浸式、AI增强的数字遗产应用提供了路径。
why_it_matters: Design Space and Implementation of RAG-B 与你的主题有弱匹配，暂时保留作低优先级跟踪。
analysis_zh:
## 核心内容
该研究探讨了如何将检索增强生成（RAG）技术与虚拟现实（VR）中的对话化身（Avatar）结合，用于考古学信息的交互式传播。研究者定义了一个设计空间，并以公元4世纪的马森提乌斯陵墓为实例，构建了一个能够基于学术文献回答用户问题的虚拟导览系统。

## 为什么相关
相关度较低（1.4）。该论文属于AI代理（Agent）与RAG技术在文化遗产领域的垂直应用。对于病原生信或临床微生物学领域，其相关性仅在于展示了如何将专业学术文本（Scholarly texts）转化为可交互的知识库，这种RAG架构在处理临床指南或病原体数据库的自动化咨询时具有潜在的工程参考价值，但领域跨度极大。

## 新颖度判断
新颖度较低（0.5）。RAG与VR化身的结合在人机交互领域已有相关探索。本研究的新颖性主要体现在针对考古学这一特定场景提出了“需求设计空间”，并利用学术文本元数据来增强RAG系统的表现，而非算法层面的根本创新。

## 方法与证据
1. **系统实现**：在VR环境中部署RAG系统，数据源为经过元数据增强的学术文本。
2. **案例研究**：针对马森提乌斯陵墓（Maxentius mausoleum）进行数字化重建与知识库构建。
3. **评估指标**：使用专家编写的小型问答集（Small expert-crafted QA set）评估回答质量；通过用户实验测量感知工作量（Perceived workload）。
4. **结论证据**：实验显示用户交互工作量低于平均水平，且化身能有效提升用户对考古主题的参与度。

## 应用价值
该研究证明了RAG化身在降低专业知识获取门槛方面的有效性，可应用于数字博物馆、虚拟考古教学以及复杂专业文献的沉浸式检索界面开发。

## 局限与风险
1. **评估规模**：评估所用的问答集规模较小，可能无法全面反映RAG系统在处理大规模、多歧义学术文献时的鲁棒性。
2. **幻觉问题**：摘要未详细说明如何解决LLM在考古专业知识上的幻觉（Hallucination）风险。
3. **技术细节缺失**：摘要未说明具体的LLM模型选择、向量数据库配置及检索策略。

title: OneSearch-V2: The Latent Reasoning Enhanced Self-distillation Generative Search Framework
abstract: Generative Retrieval (GR) has emerged as a promising paradigm for modern search systems. Compared to multi-stage cascaded architecture, it offers advantages such as end-to-end joint optimization and high computational efficiency. OneSearch, as a representative industrial-scale deployed generative search framework, has brought significant commercial and operational benefits. However, its inadequate understanding of complex queries, inefficient exploitation of latent user intents, and overfitting to narrow historical preferences have limited its further performance improvement. To address these challenges, we propose \textbf{OneSearch-V2}, a latent reasoning enhanced self-distillation generative search framework. It contains three key innovations: (1) a thought-augmented complex query understanding module, which enables deep query understanding and overcomes the shallow semantic matching limitations of direct inference; (2) a reasoning-internalized self-distillation training pipeline, which uncovers users' potential yet precise e-commerce intentions beyond log-fitting through implicit in-context learning; (3) a behavior preference alignment optimization system, which mitigates reward hacking arising from the single conversion metric, and addresses personal preference via direct user feedback. Extensive offline evaluations demonstrate OneSearch-V2's strong query recognition and user profiling capabilities. Online A/B tests further validate its business effectiveness, yielding +3.98\% item CTR, +3.05\% buyer conversion rate, and +2.11\% order volume. Manual evaluation further confirms gains in search experience quality, with +1.65\% in page good rate and +1.37\% in query-item relevance. More importantly, OneSearch-V2 effectively mitigates common search system issues such as information bubbles and long-tail sparsity, without incurring additional inference costs or serving latency.
abstract_zh: 生成式检索（GR）已成为现代搜索系统的有力范式。针对工业级框架 OneSearch 在复杂查询理解不足、潜在意图挖掘低效以及对历史偏好过拟合等局限性，本文提出了 OneSearch-V2，一种潜意识推理增强的自蒸馏生成式搜索框架。该框架包含三大核心创新：1) 思维增强的复杂查询理解模块，通过深度语义解析克服直接推理的浅层匹配限制；2) 推理内化的自蒸馏训练流水线，利用隐式上下文学习挖掘用户精准的电商意图；3) 行为偏好对齐优化系统，通过直接用户反馈缓解单一转化指标导致的奖励黑客问题。在线 A/B 测试显示，OneSearch-V2 使商品点击率（CTR）提升 3.98%，买家转化率提升 3.05%，订单量增长 2.11%。此外，人工评估证实页面优良率提升 1.65%，查询-商品相关性提升 1.37%。该框架在不增加推理延迟的前提下，有效缓解了信息茧房和长尾稀疏问题。
why_it_matters: 这篇工作更像方法创新，可能直接关联 metagenomics、long-read 或 pathogen identification 流程优化。
analysis_zh:
## 核心内容
OneSearch-V2 是一个针对大规模工业搜索场景设计的生成式检索（Generative Retrieval, GR）框架。它通过引入思维增强（Thought-augmented）模块、自蒸馏训练流水线和行为偏好对齐系统，解决了传统 GR 模型在处理复杂查询时的语义浅薄问题，并优化了用户意图的精准捕捉。

## 为什么相关
**弱 AI 交叉相关**。该论文主要聚焦于电商搜索优化，与病原生信或临床微生物学的直接相关性较弱。但其提出的“长尾稀疏（Long-tail sparsity）”处理方案和“复杂查询理解”方法，对于构建大规模病原体基因组数据库的高效检索系统、处理罕见病原体序列匹配具有算法迁移的参考价值。

## 新颖度判断
**新颖度较高（7.19）**。其创新点在于将大模型的“思维链”逻辑内化到检索框架中，通过“推理内化的自蒸馏”技术，在不增加推理成本和延迟的情况下，实现了类似复杂推理的效果，这在追求高并发、低延迟的工业检索领域具有显著的方法论突破。

## 方法与证据
- **方法**：采用思维增强模块进行深度查询解析；利用隐式上下文学习（Implicit In-context Learning）进行自蒸馏训练；引入直接用户反馈的行为偏好对齐优化。
- **证据**：通过在线 A/B 测试获得量化提升，包括商品 CTR（+3.98%）、转化率（+3.05%）和订单量（+2.11%）。人工评估显示页面优良率（+1.65%）和相关性（+1.37%）均有改善。实验证明该框架能有效抑制“奖励黑客（Reward hacking）”现象。

## 应用价值
在生物信息学领域，该框架的逻辑可用于优化宏基因组搜索引擎，特别是当用户输入复杂的表型描述或多重筛选条件时，通过其“思维增强”模块提升检索的精准度。同时，其对长尾数据的处理能力有助于发现数据库中低频出现的病原体序列。

## 局限与风险
摘要未说明该模型在非电商领域（如生物医学文献或基因序列）的泛化表现。此外，虽然不增加推理延迟，但“自蒸馏训练流水线”可能涉及较高的离线训练计算开销。对于临床微生物学等对准确性要求极高的领域，生成式检索可能存在的“幻觉”风险（即生成不存在的关联）在摘要中未被深入讨论。

title: Estimating Individual Tree Height and Species from UAV Imagery
abstract: Accurate estimation of forest biomass, a major carbon sink, relies heavily on tree-level traits such as height and species. Unoccupied Aerial Vehicles (UAVs) capturing high-resolution imagery from a single RGB camera offer a cost-effective and scalable approach for mapping and measuring individual trees. We introduce BIRCH-Trees, the first benchmark for individual tree height and species estimation from tree-centered UAV images, spanning three datasets: temperate forests, tropical forests, and boreal plantations. We also present DINOvTree, a unified approach using a Vision Foundation Model (VFM) backbone with task-specific heads for simultaneous height and species prediction. Through extensive evaluations on BIRCH-Trees, we compare DINOvTree against commonly used vision methods, including VFMs, as well as biological allometric equations. We find that DINOvTree achieves top overall results with accurate height predictions and competitive classification accuracy while using only 54% to 58% of the parameters of the second-best approach.
abstract_zh: 准确估算作为主要碳汇的森林生物量，高度依赖于单株树木的高度和物种等特征。利用单台 RGB 相机捕捉高分辨率图像的无人机（UAV）为单木制图和测量提供了一种具成本效益且可扩展的方法。本文推出了 BIRCH-Trees，这是首个用于从以树木为中心的无人机图像中估算单木高度和物种的基准数据集，涵盖了温带森林、热带森林和北方人工林三个数据集。此外，我们提出了 DINOvTree，这是一种统一的方法，采用视觉基础模型（VFM）作为骨干网络，并配备特定任务头，以同时进行高度和物种预测。通过在 BIRCH-Trees 上的广泛评估，我们将 DINOvTree 与包括其他 VFM 在内的常用视觉方法以及生物异速生长方程进行了对比。结果表明，DINOvTree 在实现准确高度预测和具竞争力的分类准确率的同时，其参数量仅为次优方法的 54% 至 58%，表现出卓越的综合性能。
why_it_matters: 这篇工作偏基础模型/Agent方向，可能影响病原检测任务的建模上限，值得关注其任务定义与评测设计。
analysis_zh:
## 核心内容
该研究解决了利用无人机（UAV）RGB 图像进行单株树木物理特征（高度）和生物分类（物种）自动评估的问题。主要贡献包括：1) 发布了名为 **BIRCH-Trees** 的首个多生境（温带、热带、北方人工林）单木基准数据集；2) 开发了基于视觉基础模型（VFM）的统一架构 **DINOvTree**，实现了多任务并行预测。

## 为什么相关
本研究属于**弱 AI 交叉**。虽然其直接应用领域是林业生态学，但其核心逻辑——利用视觉基础模型（VFM）对复杂自然样本进行多维度特征提取（回归任务预测高度，分类任务识别物种）——与病原微生物学中的显微图像识别、菌落形态学分析具有方法论上的相似性。特别是其在有限参数下实现高精度多任务学习的设计，对临床微生物自动化诊断模型的轻量化具有参考价值。

## 新颖度判断
**中等偏高**。新颖性体现在：1) 填补了单木尺度多任务学习基准数据集的空白（BIRCH-Trees）；2) 证明了通用视觉基础模型（VFM）在特定生物物理参数估计任务中，通过特定任务头设计，可以超越传统的生物异速生长方程（allometric equations）。

## 方法与证据
- **数据集**：BIRCH-Trees 涵盖了三种典型的森林生态系统（温带、热带、北方人工林），确保了模型的泛化能力。
- **模型架构**：DINOvTree 采用 VFM 骨干网络，通过多任务头设计同时输出连续变量（高度）和离散类别（物种）。
- **实验对比**：对比对象包括主流视觉模型、其他 VFM 以及传统的生物学公式。结果显示 DINOvTree 在性能领先的前提下，参数量仅为次优模型的 **54% 至 58%**，证明了其极高的参数效率。

## 应用价值
该研究为大规模、低成本的生态监测提供了技术支撑。在病原生信领域，这种“基础模型+轻量化任务头”的架构可借鉴用于病原体表型组学研究，例如从复杂的环境或临床样本图像中同时提取病原体种类及生长状态指标。

## 局限与风险
- **传感器局限**：摘要提到仅使用单台 RGB 相机，虽然成本低，但在密闭林冠下的高度测量精度可能受限，缺乏 LiDAR 等深度信息的补充。
- **数据偏差**：虽然涵盖了三种森林，但对于更复杂的混交林或极端环境下的表现，摘要未给出具体说明。
- **任务定义**：模型依赖于“以树为中心”的图像，这意味着在实际应用中需要预先进行精准的单木分割，摘要未提及分割步骤的误差对后续预测的影响。

title: Computational Design and Atomistic Validation of a High-Affinity VHH Nanobody Targeting the PI/RuvC Interface of Streptococcus pyogenes Cas9: A Bivalent Hub Strategy for CRISPR-Cas9 Enhancement
abstract: The CRISPR-Cas9 system has revolutionized genome engineering, yet its full therapeutic potential remains constrained by challenges in precisely modulating its activity and specificity. Here we report a fully computational end-to-end pipeline for the de novo design of a single-domain VHH nanobody (NbSpCas9-v1) targeting a structurally conserved, non-catalytic epitope at the PAM-interacting (PI) and RuvC-III interface of Streptococcus pyogenes Cas9 (SpCas9; PDB: 4UN3). Nanobody sequences were generated using BoltzGen, a generative diffusion binder design framework, and co-folded with SpCas9 using Boltz-2 to evaluate structural confidence and binding affinity. The top-ranked model (SpCas9_4UN3_Bivalent_Hub_v1) achieved a complex pLDDT of 0.8406, an aggregate score of 0.8016, and an ipTM of >0.8, indicating high confidence in the nanobody antigen interface. The designed 1,616-residue quaternary complex was subjected to 10 ns of all-atom molecular dynamics simulation using the AMBER14SB force field within the GROMACS/OpenMM framework. The complex stabilized at RMSD approximately 6 Angstrom with a radius of gyration of 39 to 44 Angstrom, confirming thermodynamic stability under physiological conditions (310 K, 0.15 M NaCl). A conserved 96.3 Angstrom inter-molecular distance between the nanobody centroid and the HNH catalytic residue H840 establishes NbSpCas9-v1 as a distal, non-inhibitory binder ideally suited for a Bivalent Hub architecture recruiting secondary effectors to the Cas9 ribonucleoprotein. These results provide a rigorous structural and dynamic foundation for experimental validation of VHH-based CRISPR-Cas9 enhancers and modulators.
abstract_zh: CRISPR-Cas9系统在基因组工程中具有革命性意义，但其活性和特异性的精确调节仍具挑战。本研究报告了一个全计算端到端流程，用于从头设计针对化脓性链球菌Cas9（SpCas9；PDB: 4UN3）PI与RuvC-III界面保守非催化表位的单域VHH纳米抗体（NbSpCas9-v1）。研究利用生成式扩散结合物设计框架BoltzGen生成序列，并使用Boltz-2进行共折叠以评估结构置信度和结合亲和力。排名最高的模型实现了0.8406的复合物pLDDT、0.8016的综合评分及大于0.8的ipTM，表明界面置信度极高。对1,616个残基的四元复合物进行了10 ns全原子分子动力学模拟，结果显示复合物在RMSD约6 Å处稳定，回转半径为39-44 Å，证实了生理条件下的热力学稳定性。纳米抗体质心与HNH催化残基H840之间保持96.3 Å的距离，表明NbSpCas9-v1是一种远端非抑制性结合物，适用于招募次级效应因子至Cas9核糖核蛋白的二价枢纽架构，为实验验证VHH类CRISPR增强器奠定了基础。
why_it_matters: 这篇工作更像方法创新，可能直接关联 metagenomics、long-read 或 pathogen identification 流程优化。
analysis_zh:
## 核心内容
该研究展示了一个全计算管线，用于设计针对化脓性链球菌（*S. pyogenes*）Cas9蛋白特定界面的VHH纳米抗体。核心产物是NbSpCas9-v1，其设计目标是结合在PI/RuvC-III界面，作为“二价枢纽（Bivalent Hub）”招募其他效应因子，而非直接抑制Cas9的切割活性。

## 为什么相关
本研究属于蛋白质工程与AI的强交叉领域。对于临床微生物学和基因组学而言，精确调控Cas9工具的活性是开发高效病原体检测工具（如基于CRISPR的诊断）和基因治疗的关键。该工作利用了最新的生成式AI模型（BoltzGen/Boltz-2），展示了如何通过计算手段优化病原体相关蛋白的调控因子。

## 新颖度判断
新颖度高（5.25）。其创新点在于：1. 采用了端到端的生成式扩散模型（BoltzGen）进行从头设计；2. 提出了“非抑制性远端结合”策略，通过96.3 Å的间距确保不干扰H840残基的催化功能，从而实现功能增强而非简单的阻断。

## 方法与证据
- **设计工具**：使用BoltzGen生成VHH序列，Boltz-2进行复合物预测。
- **评价指标**：复合物pLDDT达到0.8406，ipTM > 0.8，显示了极高的计算置信度。
- **动力学验证**：在GROMACS/OpenMM框架下使用AMBER14SB力场进行10 ns模拟。证据显示RMSD稳定在约6 Å，回转半径（Rg）在39-44 Å之间，证明了在310 K、0.15 M NaCl生理环境下的结构稳定性。
- **空间构象**：测得纳米抗体与H840催化位点距离为96.3 Å，支持其作为非抑制性支架的定位。

## 应用价值
该纳米抗体可作为分子支架，将脱氨酶、转录激活因子等次级效应物精准招募至SpCas9核糖核蛋白（RNP）上。这在开发新型碱基编辑器、表观遗传修饰工具以及增强CRISPR在复杂病原体基因组中的检测灵敏度方面具有直接应用潜力。

## 局限与风险
摘要未说明是否存在实验验证数据（如表面等离子体共振SPR或细胞内编辑效率实验）。10 ns的分子动力学模拟时间较短，可能不足以全面评估长效结合稳定性。此外，计算设计的纳米抗体在实际表达和折叠中的成功率仍需体外实验进一步证实。

title: On-device Semantic Selection Made Low Latency and Memory Efficient with Monolithic Forwarding
abstract: Semantic top-K selection with cross-encoder rerankers underpins on-device AI services, such as retrieval-augmented generation, agent memory, and personalized recommendation. However, its latency and memory demands dominate end-to-end budgets on edge hardware. Revisiting the objective of top-K selection, we reveal that only relative rankings matter, not exact per-candidate scores. We further observe sequence-level sparsity: relative rankings progressively stabilize in intermediate layers, enabling early pruning prior to completing full inference. Building on this insight, we propose monolithic forwarding and develop a training-free inference system, PRISM. By maintaining a global view of all candidates, it reduces latency through progressive cluster pruning. It also bounds peak memory usage by strategically overlapping I/O with computation via overlapped layer streaming and chunked execution. We evaluate PRISM against state-of-the-art baselines on rerankers from 0.6 B to 8 B parameters across Apple M2 and RTX 5070. PRISM consistently reduces latency by up to 89.2% and peak memory by up to 91.3% in microbenchmarks, without compromising precision. Across three real-world on-device AI applications, PRISM lowers latency by 11.6%-51.0% and peak memory by 18.6%-77.8%, demonstrating substantial improvements in efficiency and deployability.
abstract_zh: 语义 Top-K 选择结合交叉编码器（cross-encoder）重排序是检索增强生成（RAG）、智能体记忆和个性化推荐等端侧 AI 服务的核心。然而，其高延迟和内存需求限制了在边缘硬件上的部署。本研究重新审视 Top-K 选择目标，发现仅相对排名具有关键意义，而非精确的候选得分。研究观察到序列级稀疏性：相对排名在中间层即趋于稳定，从而允许在推理完成前进行早期剪枝。基于此，本文提出“整体转发”（monolithic forwarding）并开发了无需训练的推理系统 PRISM。PRISM 通过维护所有候选者的全局视图，利用渐进式聚类剪枝降低延迟，并通过重叠层流式传输与分块执行来优化 I/O 与计算重叠，限制峰值内存。在 Apple M2 和 RTX 5070 上对 0.6B 至 8B 参数的重排序模型进行评估，PRISM 在微基准测试中将延迟降低高达 89.2%，峰值内存降低 91.3%，且不损失精度。在真实端侧应用中，延迟和内存分别降低了 11.6%-51.0% 和 18.6%-77.8%。
why_it_matters: 这篇工作偏基础模型/Agent方向，可能影响病原检测任务的建模上限，值得关注其任务定义与评测设计。
analysis_zh:
## 核心内容
该研究针对端侧 AI（如 RAG 和智能体）中的语义重排序（Reranking）任务，提出了名为 PRISM 的推理系统。其核心逻辑是利用“相对排名在模型中间层即稳定”的特性，通过“整体转发”和“渐进式聚类剪枝”技术，在不完成全部模型层推理的情况下提前剔除低相关候选者，从而大幅压缩计算量和内存占用。

## 为什么相关
**弱 AI 交叉相关。** 本文虽属于计算机体系结构与基础模型推理优化领域，但其优化的“端侧 RAG”和“智能体记忆”是临床微生物学和病原基因组学走向现场化（Point-of-Care）的关键。例如，在资源受限的野外环境下，利用便携设备（如连接 MinION 测序仪的笔记本）进行大规模病原库检索时，PRISM 提供的低延迟重排序能力可显著提升物种鉴定和耐药基因检索的实时性。

## 新颖度判断
**较高。** 不同于传统的模型量化或蒸馏，PRISM 采用了“无需训练（Training-free）”的方案，通过观察交叉编码器内部层的排名稳定性（Sequence-level sparsity）来实现动态剪枝。其提出的“整体转发（Monolithic Forwarding）”打破了逐个处理候选者的传统模式，为端侧大模型推理提供了新的系统级优化思路。

## 方法与证据
- **实验对象**：参数量跨度从 0.6B 到 8B 的交叉编码器重排序模型。
- **硬件平台**：Apple M2 芯片（移动端代表）和 RTX 5070 GPU。
- **核心技术**：1. 渐进式聚类剪枝（基于中间层排名稳定性）；2. 重叠层流式传输（Overlapped layer streaming）与分块执行，用于平衡 I/O 和计算。
- **指标数据**：微基准测试中延迟最高降低 89.2%，峰值内存降低 91.3%。在实际应用场景中，延迟降低 11.6%-51.0%，内存降低 18.6%-77.8%，且明确指出“不损害精度（without compromising precision）”。

## 应用价值
对于病原生信领域，该技术可集成至移动端基因组分析助手或临床决策支持系统（CDSS）中。它允许在内存受限的平板或嵌入式设备上运行更高参数量（如 8B）的重排序模型，从而提高从海量文献或病原数据库中检索信息的准确度，同时保持秒级的响应速度。

## 局限与风险
摘要未说明该系统在处理极长序列（Long-context）或极高并发请求时的表现。此外，虽然在通用任务上精度无损，但在专业性极强的临床微生物术语检索中，中间层排名是否依然能保持高度稳定性（即是否会发生早期误删）仍需针对性验证。摘要亦未提及对不同架构（如 Transformer 变体）的泛化性细节。

title: Retrieval-Augmented Generation with Covariate Time Series
abstract: While RAG has greatly enhanced LLMs, extending this paradigm to Time-Series Foundation Models (TSFMs) remains a challenge. This is exemplified in the Predictive Maintenance of the Pressure Regulating and Shut-Off Valve (PRSOV), a high-stakes industrial scenario characterized by (1) data scarcity, (2) short transient sequences, and (3) covariate coupled dynamics. Unfortunately, existing time-series RAG approaches predominantly rely on generated static vector embeddings and learnable context augmenters, which may fail to distinguish similar regimes in such scarce, transient, and covariate coupled scenarios. To address these limitations, we propose RAG4CTS, a regime-aware, training-free RAG framework for Covariate Time-Series. Specifically, we construct a hierarchal time-series native knowledge base to enable lossless storage and physics-informed retrieval of raw historical regimes. We design a two-stage bi-weighted retrieval mechanism that aligns historical trends through point-wise and multivariate similarities. For context augmentation, we introduce an agent-driven strategy to dynamically optimize context in a self-supervised manner. Extensive experiments on PRSOV demonstrate that our framework significantly outperforms state-of-the-art baselines in prediction accuracy. The proposed system is deployed in Apache IoTDB within China Southern Airlines. Since deployment, our method has successfully identified one PRSOV fault in two months with zero false alarm.
abstract_zh: 检索增强生成（RAG）虽提升了LLM性能，但在时间序列基础模型（TSFM）中的应用仍具挑战。针对压力调节截止阀（PRSOV）预测性维护中存在的数据稀缺、短瞬态序列及协变量耦合动力学等难题，现有基于静态向量嵌入和可学习增强器的方法难以区分相似状态。为此，本文提出RAG4CTS，一种面向协变量时间序列的感知状态、无需训练的RAG框架。该框架构建了分层时间序列原生知识库，实现原始历史状态的无损存储与物理启发式检索；设计了两阶段双加权检索机制，通过点对点和多变量相似性对齐历史趋势；并引入智能体驱动策略进行自监督上下文动态优化。在PRSOV上的实验表明，该框架预测精度显著优于现有基准。目前已部署于中国南方航空的Apache IoTDB，两个月内成功识别一次故障且零误报。
why_it_matters: 这篇工作更像方法创新，可能直接关联 metagenomics、long-read 或 pathogen identification 流程优化。
analysis_zh:
## 核心内容
该研究提出了名为 **RAG4CTS** 的框架，旨在解决时间序列基础模型（TSFM）在处理具有协变量耦合、数据稀缺且序列短促的工业设备（如 PRSOV 阀门）预测性维护时的局限性。其核心是通过无损的物理启发式检索和智能体驱动的上下文优化，提升时间序列预测的准确性。

## 为什么相关
虽然该论文的应用背景是航空工业（PRSOV 故障检测），但其处理**协变量耦合时间序列（Covariate Time-Series）**的方法论与病原微生物监测、临床蛋白质组学动态分析高度相关。在临床微生物学中，病原体载量、宿主免疫指标及用药剂量同样构成复杂的协变量时间序列，且往往面临样本稀缺（Data scarcity）和瞬态变化（Transient sequences）的问题。RAG4CTS 的检索增强机制可为长读长测序实时监控或重症监护室（ICU）病原感染预警提供算法参考。

## 新颖度判断
**较高（0.75）**。不同于传统 RAG 依赖静态向量嵌入（Static vector embeddings），该方法引入了“分层原生知识库”实现无损存储，并采用了“两阶段双加权检索机制”来对齐多变量相似性。其“无需训练（Training-free）”和“智能体驱动（Agent-driven）”的自监督优化策略在时间序列 RAG 领域具有较强的方法论创新。

## 方法与证据
- **模型/框架**：RAG4CTS，包含分层原生知识库、两阶段双加权检索机制和智能体驱动的上下文增强策略。
- **数据与实验对象**：针对飞机压力调节截止阀（PRSOV）的工业传感器数据。
- **评估指标**：预测准确率（Prediction accuracy）显著优于 SOTA 基准。
- **实证证据**：系统已部署于中国南方航空的 Apache IoTDB 数据库。在两个月的实际运行中，成功识别出 1 起 PRSOV 故障，且保持了“零误报（Zero false alarm）”的记录。

## 应用价值
该技术可直接转化为临床微生物监测工具，用于处理多指标耦合的患者监测数据。其“物理启发式检索”可结合生物学先验知识，在病原体鉴定流程中优化对稀有或短序列片段的匹配精度。对于需要高可靠性、低误报率的临床决策支持系统（CDSS）具有重要的工程参考价值。

## 局限与风险
摘要未说明该框架在处理超大规模高维组学数据（如单细胞时序转录组）时的计算开销。此外，物理启发式检索依赖于对特定领域（如 PRSOV 的物理特性）的理解，将其迁移至生物学领域时，如何定义“生物学启发”的检索规则（如代谢通路约束）仍是挑战。目前仅在单一工业场景验证，生物医学领域的泛化性有待观察。

title: VQ-Jarvis: Retrieval-Augmented Video Restoration Agent with Sharp Vision and Fast Thought
abstract: Video restoration in real-world scenarios is challenged by heterogeneous degradations, where static architectures and fixed inference pipelines often fail to generalize. Recent agent-based approaches offer dynamic decision making, yet existing video restoration agents remain limited by insufficient quality perception and inefficient search strategies. We propose VQ-Jarvis, a retrieval-augmented, all-in-one intelligent video restoration agent with sharper vision and faster thought. VQ-Jarvis is designed to accurately perceive degradations and subtle differences among paired restoration results, while efficiently discovering optimal restoration trajectories. To enable sharp vision, we construct VSR-Compare, the first large-scale video paired enhancement dataset with 20K comparison pairs covering 7 degradation types, 11 enhancement operators, and diverse content domains. Based on this dataset, we train a multiple operator judge model and a degradation perception model to guide agent decisions. To achieve fast thought, we introduce a hierarchical operator scheduling strategy that adapts to video difficulty: for easy cases, optimal restoration trajectories are retrieved in a one-step manner from a retrieval-augmented generation (RAG) library; for harder cases, a step-by-step greedy search is performed to balance efficiency and accuracy. Extensive experiments demonstrate that VQ-Jarvis consistently outperforms existing methods on complex degraded videos.
abstract_zh: 现实场景中的视频修复面临异质退化的挑战，传统的静态架构和固定推理流程往往难以泛化。现有的基于智能体（Agent）的方法虽能动态决策，但在质量感知和搜索效率方面仍有局限。本文提出 VQ-Jarvis，一种检索增强的全能智能视频修复智能体。为了实现敏锐的视觉感知，研究者构建了首个大规模视频成对增强数据集 VSR-Compare，包含 20,000 个对比对，涵盖 7 种退化类型、11 种增强算子及多种内容领域。基于该数据集，训练了多算子评判模型和退化感知模型以指导智能体决策。在推理层面，引入分层算子调度策略：针对简单案例，通过检索增强生成（RAG）库一步获取最优修复路径；针对复杂案例，执行逐步贪婪搜索以平衡效率与精度。实验表明，VQ-Jarvis 在复杂退化视频上的表现持续优于现有方法。
why_it_matters: 这篇工作偏基础模型/Agent方向，可能影响病原检测任务的建模上限，值得关注其任务定义与评测设计。
analysis_zh:
## 核心内容
该研究开发了一个名为 VQ-Jarvis 的视频修复智能体，旨在解决真实世界中视频退化多样且难以预测的问题。其核心是通过集成退化感知模型、多算子评判模型以及检索增强生成（RAG）技术，实现对视频修复路径的动态、高效决策。

## 为什么相关
本研究属于**弱 AI 交叉**。虽然其直接对象是通用视频修复，但其提出的“智能体驱动的信号修复框架”对临床微生物学和病原检测具有潜在借鉴意义。例如，在显微成像、病理切片数字化或高通量测序信号去噪中，同样面临复杂的异质噪声（退化），VQ-Jarvis 的分层调度策略可用于优化生物医学图像的增强流程。

## 新颖度判断
**新颖度较高（0.75）**。其创新点在于：1. 构建了首个大规模视频成对增强数据集 VSR-Compare（20K 对）；2. 将 RAG 引入视频修复领域，通过“一步检索”解决简单退化，避免了传统 Agent 逐步搜索的冗余；3. 提出了针对视频修复任务的分层调度逻辑。

## 方法与证据
- **数据规模**：构建了 VSR-Compare 数据集，包含 20,000 个对比对，涵盖 7 种退化类型和 11 种增强算子。
- **模型架构**：包含一个退化感知模型（用于识别退化类型）和一个多算子评判模型（用于比较不同修复结果的优劣）。
- **推理策略**：采用分层调度。简单案例直接从 RAG 库检索路径；复杂案例使用贪婪搜索（Greedy Search）。
- **实验结论**：摘要指出 VQ-Jarvis 在复杂退化视频上的表现优于现有方法，但未给出具体的 PSNR 或 SSIM 等量化指标数值。

## 应用价值
在病原生物学领域，该技术可转化为针对低质量临床样本图像（如革兰氏染色、免疫荧光图像）的自动增强工具。通过 RAG 库存储已知的病原体特征修复路径，可以快速提升模糊或高噪声图像的清晰度，辅助 AI 辅助诊断（IVD）系统提高识别准确率。

## 局限与风险
- **计算开销**：对于“复杂案例”采用的逐步贪婪搜索可能导致推理延迟，在实时临床诊断场景中可能受限。
- **泛化性风险**：虽然涵盖了 7 种退化，但生物医学图像的退化（如样本制备伪影、特定波长干扰）可能不在其 VSR-Compare 数据集的 11 种算子覆盖范围内。
- **黑盒决策**：Agent 自动选择修复路径的逻辑缺乏可解释性，在医疗决策中可能存在合规性风险。

title: SpecXMaster Technical Report
abstract: Intelligent spectroscopy serves as a pivotal element in AI-driven closed-loop scientific discovery, functioning as the critical bridge between matter structure and artificial intelligence. However, conventional expert-dependent spectral interpretation encounters substantial hurdles, including susceptibility to human bias and error, dependence on limited specialized expertise, and variability across interpreters. To address these challenges, we propose SpecXMaster, an intelligent framework leveraging Agentic Reinforcement Learning (RL) for NMR molecular spectral interpretation. SpecXMaster enables automated extraction of multiplicity information from both 1H and 13C spectra directly from raw FID (free induction decay) data. This end-to-end pipeline enables fully automated interpretation of NMR spectra into chemical structures. It demonstrates superior performance across multiple public NMR interpretation benchmarks and has been refined through iterative evaluations by professional chemical spectroscopists. We believe that SpecXMaster, as a novel methodological paradigm for spectral interpretation, will have a profound impact on the organic chemistry community.
abstract_zh: 智能光谱分析是AI驱动闭环科学发现的关键环节，连接了物质结构与人工智能。然而，传统的专家依赖型光谱解析面临人为偏见、专业知识受限及解释一致性差等挑战。为此，研究者提出了SpecXMaster，这是一个利用代理强化学习（Agentic Reinforcement Learning, RL）进行核磁共振（NMR）分子光谱解析的智能框架。SpecXMaster能够直接从原始自由感应衰减（FID）数据中自动提取1H和13C光谱的多重性信息。该端到端流程实现了从NMR光谱到化学结构的完全自动化解析。实验表明，SpecXMaster在多个公开NMR解析基准测试中表现优异，并经过专业化学光谱学家的迭代评估与完善。作为一种新型的光谱解析范式，SpecXMaster将对有机化学领域产生深远影响。
why_it_matters: 这篇工作更像方法创新，可能直接关联 metagenomics、long-read 或 pathogen identification 流程优化。
analysis_zh:
## 核心内容
该研究推出了名为SpecXMaster的智能框架，旨在解决核磁共振（NMR）光谱解析中过度依赖人工专家的问题。其核心是利用代理强化学习（Agentic RL）技术，直接处理原始的自由感应衰减（FID）信号，自动提取1H和13C光谱的多重性信息，并最终实现从原始数据到化学结构的端到端自动化转换。

## 为什么相关
虽然该工作主要面向有机化学，但其技术逻辑与病原微生物的代谢组学研究高度相关。在临床微生物学中，通过NMR鉴定病原体代谢产物是结构生物学的重要手段。SpecXMaster展示的Agentic RL处理原始信号的能力，可为蛋白质组学或代谢组学中的复杂波谱自动化分析提供跨学科的算法借鉴，属于强AI交叉应用。

## 新颖度判断
新颖度较高（0.75）。其创新点在于：1. 放弃了传统的基于规则或简单深度学习的分类方法，引入了具备决策能力的代理强化学习（Agentic RL）；2. 实现了从原始FID数据（而非预处理后的峰表）到结构的端到端解析，极大降低了信息损耗。

## 方法与证据
- **模型/算法**：采用了代理强化学习（Agentic Reinforcement Learning）框架。
- **数据处理**：直接处理原始FID（Free Induction Decay）数据，涵盖1H和13C两种核磁共振谱图。
- **实验证据**：摘要提到该模型在“多个公开NMR解析基准测试”中表现优异，并经过了“专业化学光谱学家”的迭代评估，证明了其在实际专家工作流中的可靠性。

## 应用价值
该工具显著提升了化学结构鉴定的自动化水平，减少了对稀缺专家资源的依赖，并消除了人工解析中的主观偏见。在药物研发、天然产物鉴定以及病原体小分子代谢物分析中具有直接的应用潜力，能够加速从样本采集到结构确认的闭环流程。

## 局限与风险
摘要未详细说明该模型在处理复杂混合物（如临床生物样本）时的表现，通常此类样本的信号重叠严重。此外，对于非标准核磁共振环境或低场强数据的鲁棒性，以及具体涉及哪些“公开基准测试”及其指标（如准确率、召回率的具体数值），摘要中均未明确说明。

title: From Questions to Trust Reports: A LLM-IR Framework for the TREC 2025 DRAGUN Track
abstract: The DRAGUN Track at TREC 2025 targets the growing need for effective support tools that help users evaluate the trustworthiness of online news. We describe the UR_Trecking system submitted for both Task 1 (critical question generation) and Task 2 (retrieval-augmented trustworthiness reporting). Our approach combines LLM-based question generation with semantic filtering, diversity enforcement using clustering, and several query expansion strategies (including reasoning-based Chain-of-Thought expansion) to retrieve relevant evidence from the MS MARCO V2.1 segmented corpus. Retrieved documents are re-ranked using a monoT5 model and filtered using an LLM relevance judge together with a domain-level trustworthiness dataset. For Task 2, selected evidence is synthesized by an LLM into concise trustworthiness reports with citations. Results from the official evaluation indicate that Chain-of-Thought query expansion and re-ranking substantially improve both relevance and domain trust compared to baseline retrieval, while question-generation performance shows moderate quality with room for improvement. We conclude by outlining key challenges encountered and suggesting directions for enhancing robustness and trustworthiness assessment in future iterations of the system.
abstract_zh: TREC 2025 DRAGUN 赛道旨在开发评估在线新闻可信度的支持工具。本文介绍了 UR_Trecking 系统，该系统参与了任务 1（关键问题生成）和任务 2（检索增强的可信度报告）。该方法结合了基于大语言模型（LLM）的问题生成与语义过滤，利用聚类实现多样性增强，并采用多种查询扩展策略（包括基于推理的思维链 CoT 扩展），从 MS MARCO V2.1 分段语料库中检索证据。检索到的文档通过 monoT5 模型重排序，并结合 LLM 相关性评判及领域级可信度数据集进行过滤。在任务 2 中，LLM 将选定的证据合成为带有引用的简明可信度报告。官方评估结果显示，与基准检索相比，CoT 查询扩展和重排序显著提升了相关性和领域信任度，而问题生成的表现尚有提升空间。研究总结了系统鲁棒性和可信度评估面临的挑战。
why_it_matters: 这篇工作偏数据集或基准构建，适合判断是否能作为病原组学训练或评测资源。
analysis_zh:
## 核心内容
该研究展示了一个名为 UR_Trecking 的 LLM-IR（大语言模型与信息检索）框架，专门用于 TREC 2025 DRAGUN 赛道。其核心任务是针对在线信息生成关键性提问，并基于检索到的证据生成带有引用的可信度评估报告。系统集成了问题生成、语义过滤、基于思维链（CoT）的查询扩展、monoT5 重排序以及 LLM 证据合成等模块。

## 为什么相关
**弱相关。** 本文主要针对在线新闻的可信度评估，而非直接针对病原组学或临床微生物学。然而，其核心逻辑——“从海量语料中检索证据并生成可信的合成报告”——与临床微生物学中“基于文献证据进行病原体致病性分析”或“耐药性临床决策支持”的 RAG（检索增强生成）需求高度相似。对于需要高度严谨性的临床领域，这种“可信度报告”框架具有方法论参考价值。

## 新颖度判断
**中等偏高。** 该工作的新颖之处在于将思维链（CoT）推理应用于查询扩展，并结合了领域级可信度数据集进行过滤。在 TREC 竞赛背景下，这种将传统 IR 模型（如 monoT5）与现代 LLM 评判器结合的多阶段流水线，代表了当前检索增强生成领域的前沿实践。

## 方法与证据
- **数据规模与来源**：使用了 MS MARCO V2.1 分段语料库作为检索源，并结合了特定的领域级可信度数据集。
- **技术栈**：采用 LLM 进行问题生成；使用聚类算法确保问题多样性；利用 CoT 进行查询扩展；使用 monoT5 模型进行文档重排序。
- **实验结论**：官方评估显示，CoT 查询扩展和重排序显著改善了检索的相关性和领域信任指标，优于基准（Baseline）检索方法。但摘要指出问题生成任务（Task 1）的质量仅为“中等”，仍有改进空间。

## 应用价值
该框架可迁移至临床微生物学领域，用于构建“病原体知识信任报告系统”。例如，当临床医生询问某种罕见菌株的致病性时，系统可以模仿该框架：生成关键临床问题、从 PubMed 等数据库检索证据、通过 monoT5 筛选高质量文献，最后生成带有引用文献的可信度评估报告，减少 LLM 的幻觉风险。

## 局限与风险
- **鲁棒性不足**：摘要明确提到系统在鲁棒性方面存在挑战。
- **生成质量波动**：问题生成模块的表现尚未达到理想水平，可能导致后续检索偏离核心目标。
- **领域局限**：目前仅在新闻语料（MS MARCO）上验证，迁移至专业性极强的医学或基因组学领域时，可能面临专业术语理解不足和证据权重判定困难的问题。

title: Agentic Personas for Adaptive Scientific Explanations with Knowledge Graphs
abstract: AI explanation methods often assume a static user model, producing non-adaptive explanations regardless of expert goals, reasoning strategies, or decision contexts. Knowledge graph-based explanations, despite their capacity for grounded, path-based reasoning, inherit this limitation. In complex domains such as scientific discovery, this assumption fails to capture the diversity of cognitive strategies and epistemic stances among experts, preventing explanations that foster deeper understanding and informed decision-making. However, the scarcity of human experts limits the use of direct human feedback to produce adaptive explanations. We present a reinforcement learning approach for scientific explanation generation that incorporates agentic personas, structured representations of expert reasoning strategies, that guide the explanation agent towards specific epistemic preferences. In an evaluation of knowledge graph-based explanations for drug discovery, we tested two personas that capture distinct epistemic stances derived from expert feedback. Results show that persona-driven explanations match state-of-the-art predictive performance while persona preferences closely align with those of their corresponding experts. Adaptive explanations were consistently preferred over non-adaptive baselines (n = 22), and persona-based training reduces feedback requirements by two orders of magnitude. These findings demonstrate how agentic personas enable scalable adaptive explainability for AI systems in complex and high-stakes domains.
abstract_zh: 现有的AI解释方法通常假设用户模型是静态的，无法根据专家的目标、推理策略或决策背景生成自适应解释。基于知识图谱（KG）的解释虽然具备基于路径的推理能力，但也存在这一局限。在科学发现等复杂领域，这种假设忽略了专家认知策略的多样性，阻碍了深度理解。由于人类专家稀缺，直接利用人类反馈生成自适应解释受到限制。本研究提出一种用于科学解释生成的强化学习方法，通过引入“智能体人格”（agentic personas）——即专家推理策略的结构化表示，引导解释智能体趋向特定的认识偏好。在药物研发领域的知识图谱解释评估中，研究测试了两种源自专家反馈的认识立场人格。结果显示，人格驱动的解释在预测性能上达到了先进水平，且人格偏好与对应专家高度一致。在与非自适应基准的对比中（n=22），自适应解释更受青睐，且基于人格的训练将反馈需求降低了两个数量级。这表明智能体人格能为复杂高风险领域的AI系统提供可扩展的自适应解释能力。
why_it_matters: 这篇工作偏基础模型/Agent方向，可能影响病原检测任务的建模上限，值得关注其任务定义与评测设计。
analysis_zh:
## 核心内容
该研究针对科学发现领域中AI解释过于僵化的问题，提出了一种结合强化学习（RL）与“智能体人格”（Agentic Personas）的框架。该框架利用知识图谱（KG）进行路径推理，并通过结构化的专家推理策略（人格）来定制化解释输出，使其能够适应不同专家的认知需求和决策背景。

## 为什么相关
在病原检测、临床微生物学及基因组学领域，AI给出的结论（如致病性预测或药物敏感性建议）往往需要结合复杂的生物学机理。传统的“一刀切”解释难以满足不同背景专家（如临床医生与生物信息学家）的需求。该研究提出的自适应解释机制，对于提高病原检测任务中AI决策的可信度和透明度具有参考意义。

## 新颖度判断
新颖度中等（0.25）。其创新点在于将“人格”概念引入科学解释的强化学习过程中，解决了专家反馈数据稀缺的痛点。通过将专家的认识立场结构化，实现了在极少人类干预下生成符合特定逻辑偏好的解释，这在知识图谱推理领域具有一定的启发性。

## 方法与证据
- **方法**：采用强化学习方法，将“智能体人格”作为结构化约束，引导解释智能体在知识图谱上进行路径搜索。
- **实验对象**：药物研发（Drug Discovery）领域的知识图谱解释任务。
- **证据指标**：
    - 性能：人格驱动的解释保持了最先进（SOTA）的预测准确率。
    - 用户偏好：在 n=22 的对比实验中，自适应解释优于非自适应基准。
    - 效率：基于人格的训练方法将所需的人类反馈量降低了两个数量级（100倍）。

## 应用价值
该方法在临床微生物学和药物筛选等高风险领域具有显著应用潜力。它允许系统根据使用者的专业背景（如更关注分子机制或更关注临床统计证据）动态调整解释侧重点。同时，大幅降低对人类专家标注依赖的特性，使得该技术在数据匮乏的细分病原研究领域更易落地。

## 局限与风险
- **样本规模**：摘要中提到的用户评估样本量较小（n=22），其结论的普适性有待在大规模临床场景中验证。
- **人格覆盖度**：实验仅测试了两种人格立场，摘要未说明该方法是否能有效扩展到更复杂、更多样化的专家思维模型中。
- **领域局限**：目前仅在药物研发领域验证，尚未在病原基因组学或蛋白质组学等更具异构性的数据上进行测试。

title: Graph-Aware Late Chunking for Retrieval-Augmented Generation in Biomedical Literature
abstract: Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) systems for biomedical literature are typically evaluated using ranking metrics like Mean Reciprocal Rank (MRR), which measure how well the system identifies the single most relevant chunk. We argue that for full-text scientific documents, this paradigm is incomplete: it rewards retrieval precision while ignoring retrieval breadth -- the ability to surface evidence from across a document's structural sections. We propose GraLC-RAG, a framework that unifies late chunking with graph-aware structural intelligence, introducing structure-aware chunk boundary detection, UMLS knowledge graph infusion, and graph-guided hybrid retrieval. We evaluate six strategies on 2,359 IMRaD-filtered PubMed Central articles using 2,033 cross-section questions and two metric families: standard ranking metrics (MRR, Recall@k) and structural coverage metrics (SecCov@k, CS Recall). Our results expose a sharp divergence: content-similarity methods achieve the highest MRR (0.517) but always retrieve from a single section, while structure-aware methods retrieve from up to 15.6x more sections. Generation experiments show that KG-infused retrieval narrows the answer-quality gap to delta-F1 = 0.009 while maintaining 4.6x section diversity. These findings demonstrate that standard metrics systematically undervalue structural retrieval and that closing the multi-section synthesis gap is a key open problem for biomedical RAG.
abstract_zh: 生物医学文献的检索增强生成（RAG）系统通常使用平均倒数排名（MRR）等指标评估，侧重于识别单个最相关分块，但这忽略了从文档不同结构章节中提取证据的检索广度。本研究提出 GraLC-RAG 框架，将延迟分块（late chunking）与图感知结构智能相结合，引入了结构感知分块边界检测、UMLS 知识图谱注入和图引导混合检索。研究在 2,359 篇经 IMRaD 筛选的 PubMed Central 文章上，利用 2,033 个跨章节问题进行了评估。结果显示，内容相似度方法虽获得最高 MRR（0.517），但检索结果局限于单一章节；而结构感知方法检索到的章节数量多出 15.6 倍。生成实验表明，注入知识图谱（KG）的检索在保持 4.6 倍章节多样性的同时，将答案质量差距缩小至 delta-F1 = 0.009。这表明标准指标低估了结构化检索的重要性，解决多章节综合差距是生物医学 RAG 的关键。
why_it_matters: 这篇工作更像方法创新，可能直接关联 metagenomics、long-read 或 pathogen identification 流程优化。
analysis_zh:
## 核心内容
该研究针对生物医学 RAG 系统中“检索精度高但广度不足”的问题，提出了 GraLC-RAG 框架。其核心是通过延迟分块（Late Chunking）结合文档结构感知和 UMLS 知识图谱，提升系统从全文（如 IMRaD 结构的论文）中跨章节提取证据的能力，而非仅仅定位到单个最相似的文本块。

## 为什么相关
在病原体基因组学或临床微生物学研究中，关键信息往往分散在不同章节：例如“方法”中的测序参数、“结果”中的耐药表型数据与“讨论”中的流行病学意义。传统的 RAG 往往只能检索到其中一处，而 GraLC-RAG 提出的跨章节检索能力对于构建能够自动总结病原体特征、合成临床报告的 AI Agent 具有直接的技术参考价值。

## 新颖度判断
新颖度中等（0.25）。该工作的主要创新点在于将“延迟分块”这一新兴技术与生物医学特有的 UMLS 知识图谱及文档结构（IMRaD）相结合，并提出了专门衡量结构覆盖度的指标（SecCov@k 和 CS Recall），而非单纯依赖传统的 MRR 指标。

## 方法与证据
研究使用了 2,359 篇 PubMed Central 文章和 2,033 个跨章节问题进行基准测试。实验对比了六种策略，证据显示：虽然基于内容相似度的传统方法 MRR 达到 0.517，但其检索范围极窄；相比之下，结构感知方法检索到的章节数量提升了 15.6 倍。在生成质量上，通过注入 UMLS 知识图谱，系统在保持 4.6 倍章节多样性的前提下，将 F1 分数差距缩小到了极小的 0.009。

## 应用价值
该方法可直接优化生物医学文献挖掘工具。对于需要处理长文本（如三代测序分析报告或复杂的临床微生物学综述）的 RAG 系统，GraLC-RAG 提供的结构感知边界检测和图引导检索能显著提升回答的全面性，避免 AI 遗漏文档深处的关键实验细节。

## 局限与风险
摘要指出，内容相似度方法在 MRR 指标上依然占优（0.517），这暗示了结构感知方法在牺牲部分局部检索精度来换取全局覆盖度。此外，多章节信息的综合（Synthesis Gap）仍被定义为一个“开放性问题”，说明模型在获取多点证据后，如何完美地将其融合成准确答案仍存在挑战。摘要未说明该框架在大规模实时检索中的计算开销。
